channel.title: Bash Script on Mr.Tinkerer's Blogs
channel.description: Recent content in Bash Script on Mr.Tinkerer's Blogs

item.title: How I Streamline the Creation of Blogs
item.description:
The Issue

So I have written the two blogs (The Laptop Proxmox Blog, and Laptop NAS build), but I don’t have a way to share them. I have written in markdown file (via Obsidian), since it would be easy for me to write them. The problem is that you can’t just put a markdown file into a web server and expect it to work. So I would need to find a way to convert them into HTML. So after a some research, I have developed a plan.

item.title: Turning the Laptop into a NAS
item.description:
The NAS Plan

So recently, I have successfully turned an old laptop into a Proxmox server. However, I have only got the OS installed on the laptop. No VMs, and no LXCs containers on there. So this blog will be me changing that by stetting up a NAS (Network Attached Storage).

Now there are many options for me to use for the NAS software. I could go with a NAS OS that has all of the tools setup like TrueNAS. Or I could install a Linux Distro and manually install & setup the tools. I have decided to go with OpenMediaVault (OMV). The reason why is that I didn’t want to manually create the NAS from a linux distro (yet). However, going with something like TrueNAS wouldn’t be possible, as their minimum requirements would take up most of the resources of the laptop.
